summary: The Netherlands runs the central-government programme Quantumveilige Cryptografie, with the intelligence service AIVD, together with CWI and TNO, publishing the PQC Migration Handbook, and NCSC-NL providing guidance for organisations starting their migration. Dutch guidance follows the three post-quantum standards published by NIST.
govActors:
AIVD | General Intelligence and Security Service, co-author with CWI and TNO of the PQC Migration Handbook and statements on the transition
NCSC-NL | National Cyber Security Centre, explainers and guidance for organisations starting a quantum migration programme
BZK | Ministry of the Interior and Kingdom Relations, letter to Parliament on the renewed quantum-safe cryptography handbook and the PQChoiceAssistant tool
Rijksoverheid | Dutch central government, the Quantumveilige Cryptografie Rijk programme and the PQC symposium
Tweede Kamer | House of Representatives, answers to parliamentary questions on quantum-proof encryption
provenance: summary + govActors (AIVD, NCSC-NL, BZK, Rijksoverheid): PQC Migration Handbook, NCSC-NL guidance rows, BZK letter to Parliament, and Rijksoverheid programme rows
dominantProcess NIST: NCSC-NL expert blog on NIST's publication of three post-quantum cryptography standards (13 August 2024) row. No Dutch sovereign scheme stated, so NIST assigned
Tweede Kamer role: parliamentary questions and EU quantum strategy document rows

standardFamilies / algorithms: OMITTED. The NCSC-NL row attests that NIST released three standards but the included summaries do not name FIPS 203/204/205 or ML-KEM/ML-DSA/SLH-DSA explicitly - REVIEW handbook for named algorithms

migrationTimeline / targetCompletion: OMITTED, no dated migration milestones or end date stated in the included summaries (AIVD statements warn migration may not finish in time but give no fixed date)
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
